channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Who's to blame for USMNT's World Cup exit? Rating ...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: This USMNT was supposed to be special, but terrible performances throughout the U.S. roster led to a bitter World Cup exit in the round of 16.

item.title: Belgium handed U.S. a World Cup reality check -- a...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The U.S.'s World Cup dream went up in smoke on Monday, comprehensively dismantled 4-1 by Belgium.

item.title: Poch mum on U.S. future; tells fans to keep faith
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino would not offer an indication about whether he wants to remain as coach of the United States men's national team following the Americans' exit from the World Cup here on Monday.

item.title: Scaloni: 'No truly dominant team' at World Cup
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni said the 2026 World Cup is presenting a strong challenge for the tournament favorites, who are struggling to overcome lower-ranked opponents

item.title: USMNT's World Cup dream crushed by Belgium
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Even with Folarin Balogun back up front, the United States men's national team crashed out of the World Cup in the round of 16 again.

item.title: Ronaldo exits in tears as Spain end final World Cu...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: An emotional Cristiano Ronaldo said he had a "clear conscience" as he exited his final World Cup as Portugal were eliminated 1-0 by Spain in the round of 16 in Arlington, Texas, on Monday.

item.title: Mbappé: Paraguay senator remarks spread racism
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: France star Kylian Mbappé on Monday spoke out against racist comments made by a Paraguay senator.

item.title: 🏆2026-27 UCL season begins mid-World Cup
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The new Champions League campaign begins 38 days after last season's final. Plenty of unheralded clubs are involved, all with their own fascinating stories.

item.title: Pulisic forced off injured for USMNT vs. Belgium
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Christian Pulisic was forced off with an ankle injury in the second half of the United States' World Cup round of 16 loss against Belgium.

item.title: MVP Rodri shines, seeks more from Spain in QFs
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Spain midfielder Rodri was named the best player in his team's 1-0 victory over Portugal but warned they still have "room for improvement" ahead of the World Cup quarterfinals

item.title: Giuliani: FIFA review won't affect USMNT legacy
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the head of the White House task force on the World Cup, told ESPN on Monday that he has no concerns at all over FIFA's decision to reduce Folarin Balogun's suspension affecting the legacy or credibility of what the USMNT accomplishes.

item.title: How blind UNILAG freshman beat brain tumour to top JAMB
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Meet Ogunsua-Dixon Tijesuni, the blind UNILAG freshman who overcame a brain tumour and multiple surgeries to emerge as the top JAMB candidate in the disabi
Read More: https://punchng.com/how-blind-unilag-freshman-beat-brain-tumour-to-top-jamb/

item.title: Mercy Eke confesses to getting a BBL due to peer pressure
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: She also added that although the procedure was painful, she is happy with the outcome and now feels comfortable with her body.

item.title: US Talks Tested as LNG Ship Struck in Hormuz Strait
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A laden liquefied natural gas carrier was hit by a projectile near the Omani coast as it exited the Strait of Hormuz, heightening unease among shipowners and testing a US-Iran agreement intended to halt attacks in the waterway. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace breaks down the situation. (Sourc

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup career ends as Spain defeat Portugal
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo left the pitch for the last time at a World Cup, after Spain beat Portugal 1-0.

item.title: Mbappé Condemns Paraguayan Senator Over Racist Abuse After World Cup Match
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Kylian Mbappé on Monday labelled a Paraguayan senator “despicable” and “not worthy of her position” after she racially abused the France captain on social media following the World Cup last-16 clash between the countries at the weekend. Mbappé’s reaction came after Cele

item.title: The flawed logic behind FTSE Russell’s decision on Nigeria
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: When FTSE Russell announced its proposal to reclassify Nigeria from “Unclassified” to “Frontier Market” status, effective 21 September 2026, many read more The flawed logic behind FTSE Russell’s decision on Nigeria

item.title: W/Cup: Belgium Thrash USA 4-1, Reach Quarterfinals To Face Spain
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Belgium produced a dominant display to defeat co-hosts the United States 4-1 on Tuesday, booking a place in the quarterfinals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup where they will face Spain. Charles De Ketelaere scored twice as the Red Devils outclassed Mauricio Pochettino’s side in Seattle, ending the

item.title: AI and the rise of the ‘Efiko’

item.description: Growing up in Nigeria, there were two labels every student understood. The “Efiko” was the serious student – the one read more AI and the rise of the ‘Efiko’

item.title: India Property Developer K Raheja Said to Delay $700 Million IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: K Raheja Corp. Pvt., one of India’s largest real estate developers, has delayed plans for an initial public offering after feedback from investment bankers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Policing, electricity and Nigeria’s bipolar federalism
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: Nigeria speaks federalism fluently but practises it reluctantly. We know the catchphrases: ‘true federalism’, ‘restructuring’, and ‘devolution’. It surfaces when read more Policing, electricity and Nigeria’s bipolar federalism

item.title: Ibadan Province Lions Club provides borehole to Akinware community
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Residents of Akinware community in Ido Local Government Area of Oyo State have celebrated the inauguration of a solar-powered borehole donated by the Ibadan Providence Lions Club, ending years of inadequate access to potable water. The post Ibadan Province Lions Club provides borehole to Akinware co

item.title: Zelensky to press Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The Ukrainian president will use the Nato meeting in Turkey to argue he needs more interceptor missiles.

item.title: Martinez Resigns As Portugal Head Coach After World Cup Exit
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Roberto Martinez confirmed that he is leaving his job as Portugal coach after Monday’s 1-0 FIFA World Cup round-of-16 defeat to Spain, while denying that the result was “a failure.” Substitute Mikel Merino scored in the 91st minute at AT&T Stadium to see Spain progress to the

item.title: 15 persons burnt to death in Niger over land dispute
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tragedy strikes Niger State as 15 persons are burnt to death and three others shot in a brutal land dispute in Rafi LGA. Investigations are ongoing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/15-persons-burnt-to-death-in-niger-over-land-dispute/

item.title: Europe’s Defense Stocks Rally Has Much Riding on NATO Summit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A fledgling rebound in European defense stocks faces a test this week, as investors look to a NATO summit for further clarity on member states’ defense spending plans.

item.title: Ondo Police Raise Alarm, Mobilise Security Over Planned ‘7/7’ Cult Activities
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has raised the alarm over an alleged planned "7/7" cult celebration across the state, warning that it will not tolerate any unlawful gathering, procession, initiation or other activities capable of threatening public peace. item.title: Bathing with rainwater may trigger skin infection, physicians tell Nigerians

item.description:

item.description: The country has been hit by damaging thunderstorms, leaving at least 275 people injured.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran's TV claims the tanker ignored warnings, but no direct responsibility for the attack has been declared.

item.title: Portugal’s Euro 2016 Title Win Holds Same Weight As World Cup —Ronaldo
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has argued that the 2016 European Championship that Portugal won holds the same weight as the World Cup. Ronaldo featured in his sixth World Cup in North America this summer, and his three goals meant he became the first player to score in six World Cups. Despite his stellar record

item.title: Reforming the NYSC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THERE is hardly any Nigerian who would argue that the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) should remain frozen in time. Institutions, like nations, must evolve to remain relevant. The post Reforming the NYSC appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Mystery Thai Investor’s Near $1 Billion True Stake Sparks Probe
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Thailand’s market regulator is probing the sudden emergence of an individual shareholder who has amassed nearly $1 billion worth of shares in True Corp., making her one of the telecom giant’s largest shareholders.

item.description: The period of vertical ascent is probably over.

item.title: Japan’s Inpex Signs 15-Year Deal to Buy Persian Gulf LNG
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japan’s state-backed Inpex Corp. signed a 15-year deal with Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. for the supply of liquefied natural gas from its project in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Japan 30-Year Bonds Rise After Strong Demand Reassures Investors
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japan’s 30-year government bonds rose after an auction of the tenor drew its strongest demand since 2019, as high yield levels attracted investors despite worries about fiscal policy and inflation.

item.title: NGE to host 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture July 23
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) will on July 23, 2026 host the 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture in Lagos. The Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture is an annual lecture series instituted by the NGE in 2023 to honour the legacy of Alhaji Lateef Jakande, the late iconic journalist, who was one-time P

item.title: FG queries high petrol costs amid falling global crude prices
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The federal government, on Monday, convened a high-level meeting with key stakeholders in the downstream petroleum sector to address concerns over the pricing of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), insisting that the benefits of lower global crude oil prices should be reflected in pump prices. item.title: Tony Elumelu to retire as UBA Chairman Aug 21, Emmanuel Nnorom named successor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) has announced the retirement of its Group Chairman, Tony Elumelu, with effect from August 21, 2026, after completing the 12-year tenure limit for non-executive directors stipulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria. item.dc:creator: The Sun

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has reiterated its commitment to evacuating the fourth batch of Nigerians from South Africa, in the bid to ensure their safety and welfare. The post Xenophobia: FG to evacuate more Nigerians appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: US Loses to Belgium in World Cup Match Amid Controversy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Team USA’s run in the World Cup came to an end on Monday as it failed to make the quarterfinals, while outrage over FIFA’s decision to allow striker Folarin Balogun to play continued to simmer. Bloomberg's Balazs Penz breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.description: From Ndubuisi Orji and Adesuwa Tsan, Abuja The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has said it is targeting a revenue of N11.07 trillion for the 2026 fiscal, noting that it would deepen automation, expand intelligence-led enforcement and enhance trade facilitation to achieve its projection. item.description: The hearing will decide whether Tyler Robinson will stand trial in the shooting death of Kirk, a key Trump ally.

item.description: Kogi State governor, Alhaji Usman Ododo, has applauded the people of Ekinrin-Adde in Ijumu Local Government Area of the state for their commitment to grassroots development through self-help initiatives aimed at improving the welfare of residents. The post Ododo hails Ekinrin-Adde’s self-help develo

item.title: Belgium Crush Co-host USA To Reach World Cup Quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Belgium produced a ruthless attacking display to end co-hosts the United States’ FIFA World Cup dream with an emphatic 4-1 victory in their Round of 16 clash, booking a quarter-final showdown with Spain. The Red Devils proved far too clinical for the Americans, with Charles De Ketelaere scoring twic

item.title: Balogun reprieve in vain as Belgium beat USA to set up Spain quarterfinal
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Folarin Balogun's starting place, after a suspended red card, unable to influence USA, who succumb to superior Belgium.

item.title: Alleged anti-media practices: FG orders FCCPC to probe X, Meta, AI companies
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to a statement issued by the FCCPC on Monday, the directive was conveyed in a letter signed by the Minister of Information and National Orientation, Mohammed Idris, requesting the commission to investigate the allegations contained in the petition. The post Alleged anti-media practices: FG

item.title: FEPPPAN lauds Tinubu over N39bn pension liabilities settlement, hails NITEL/MTEL arrears liquidation
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Federal Parastatals and Private Sector Pensioners Association of Nigeria (FEPPPAN) has applauded President Bola Tinubu, for approving the liquidation of more than N39 billion inherited Defined Benefit Scheme (DBS) pension liabilities The post FEPPPAN lauds Tinubu over N39bn pension liabilities s

item.title: World Cup 2026: Belgium thump USA to reach quarter-finals and knock co-hosts out of tournament
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 'Total calamity!' US gift Belgium a third goal
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: USA goalkeeper Matt Freese with a huge error in judgement as he gifts Belgium's Hans Vanaken the ball, leaving him with an easy finish that defender Tim Ream could've done more to stop. Errors all around as Belgium take control of the round of 16 tie.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes death toll jumps to more than 3,500
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Experts warn of an impending health crisis, with thousands sleeping in crowded temporary shelters.
